title: Adhesive pad and hemostasis system
abstract: An adhesive pad for use with a hemostasis device comprising a footplate having a top surface and a receiving device positioned on the footplate, the adhesive pad comprising: (1) a flexible backing layer having a bottom surface, a central portion having an opening, a first side portion, and a second side portion; (2) an adhesive layer applied to the bottom surface of the backing layer; and (3) a release layer removably secured to the adhesive layer. The opening in the central portion of the backing layer is sized to fit the receiving device of the hemostasis device, and the adhesive layer applied to the bottom surface of the first and second side portions of the backing layer is configured to adhere to and extend beyond the top surface of the footplate.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An adhesive pad for use with a hemostasis device comprising a footplate having a top surface, and a receiving device positioned on the footplate, the adhesive pad comprising: a flexible backing layer having a bottom surface, a central portion having an opening, a first side portion, and a second side portion; an adhesive layer applied to the bottom surface of the backing layer; and a release layer removably secured to the adhesive layer; wherein the opening in the central portion of the backing layer is sized to fit the receiving device of the hemostasis device, and wherein the adhesive layer applied to the bottom surface of the first and second side portions of the backing layer is configured to adhere to and extend beyond the top surface of the footplate. 2. The adhesive pad of claim 1, wherein the backing layer comprises paper, woven fabric, or non-woven plastic film. 3. The adhesive pad of claim 1, wherein the backing layer comprises a non-woven plastic film. 4. The adhesive pad of claim 3, wherein the non-woven plastic film is selected from the group consisting of polyethylene, polypropylene, polyurethane, polyester, poly(ethylene-vinyl acetate), and poly(vinyl chloride). 5. The adhesive pad of claim 1, wherein the release layer has an opening coincident with the opening in the central portion of the backing layer. 6. The adhesive pad of claim 1, wherein the release liner comprises a plurality of separate sections. 7. The adhesive pad of claim 1, wherein the release liner is paper. 8 A hemostasis system for adhering to a patient's skin and applying a compressive force to a puncture site, the system comprising: a hemostasis device comprising a footplate having a bottom surface and a receiving device positioned on the footplate; and an adhesive pad comprising a flexible backing layer with a bottom surface, a central portion having an opening, a first side portion, and a second side portion; an adhesive layer applied to the bottom surface of the backing layer; and a release liner removably secured to the adhesive layer; wherein the central portion of the backing layer of the adhesive pad is sized to fit the receiving device of the hemostasis device, and wherein the first and second side portions of the backing layer are configured to adhere to and extend beyond the top surface of the footplate of the hemostasis device. 9. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein the backing layer of the pad comprises paper, woven fabric, or non-woven plastic film. 10. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein the backing layer of the pad comprises a non- woven plastic film. 11. The hemostasis system of claim 10, wherein the non-woven plastic film is selected from the group consisting of polyethylene, polypropylene, polyurethane, polyester, polyethylene- vinyl acetate), and poly(vinyl chloride). 12. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein the release liner of the pad has an opening coincident with the opening in the central portion of the backing layer. 13. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein the release liner of the pad comprises a plurality of separate sections. 14. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein the release liner of the pad is paper. 15. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein the plunger of the hemostasis device further comprises a compression pad. 16. The hemostasis system of claim 8, wherein one or more adhesive pads are adhered to the bottom surface of the footplate. 17. A method for applying a compressive force to a patient's skin at the site of a puncture site, the method comprising the steps of: placing a hemostasis device on the patient's skin at the site of a puncture site, wherein the hemostasis device comprises a footplate having a top surface, a receiving device positioned on the footplate, a plunger positioned within the receiving device and configured to move therethrough, and a plurality of motion restricting components interposed between the receiving device and the plunger; adhesively anchoring an adhesive pad to the hemostasis device and to the patient's skin proximal to the puncture site, wherein the adhesive pad comprises a flexible backing layer having a bottom surface, a central portion having an opening sized to fit the receiving device of the hemostasis device, a first side portion, a second side portion, and an adhesive layer applied to the bottom surface of the backing layer, wherein the first side portion and the second side portion of the backing layer overlie and adhere to the top surface of the footplate of the hemostasis device and to the patient's skin proximal to the puncture site; advancing the plunger through the receiving device until the plunger applies a compressive force to the puncture site; and engaging the plurality of motion restricting components to restrict reverse movement of the plunger through the receiving device.
